skill.name: Conductor Setup
skill.description: Initialize or resume Conductor project setup through interactive Q&A. Creates foundational project documentation including product definition, tech stack, workflow preferences, and code style guides.
skill.summary: Initialize Conductor project documentation through guided interactive Q&A
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. The skill uses interactive Q&A to guide users through creating project documentation files. No network requests, file system access beyond creating documentation, or external command execution. Static analysis confirmed 0 files scanned with 0 potential security issues. Risk score: 0/100.

content.value_statement: Projects often lack consistent documentation structure. This skill guides users through interactive Q&A to generate standardized Conductor documentation including product definition, tech stack, workflow, and code style guides.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through interactive Q&A to define project properties
Detect project type (greenfield or brownfield) and pre-populate tech stack
Generate documentation artifacts: index.md, product.md, tech-stack.md, workflow.md
Support resume functionality for interrupted setup sessions
Create code style guides for detected programming languages
limitations: Does not execute code or modify existing source files
Cannot detect all project frameworks without explicit user input
Limited to documentation generation only, no actual project scaffolding guides are
Style template-based, not tailored to specific project needs

use_cases.title: New Project Initialization
use_cases.description: Starting a new project and want to establish consistent documentation from the beginning

use_cases.title: Existing Project Documentation
use_cases.description: Adding structure to an existing brownfield project with tech stack detection

use_cases.title: Team Convention Standardization
use_cases.description: Establishing team-wide conventions for workflow, code review, and style guides

prompt_templates.title: Resume Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /conductor-setup --resume to continue from where I left off

prompt_templates.title: Project Type Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /conductor-setup and detect my existing tech stack from package.json and requirements.txt
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up docs for existing project

prompt_templates.title: Style Guide Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /conductor-setup and generate TypeScript and Python style guides

output_examples.output: Conductor setup complete! Created artifacts: conductor/index.md, conductor/product.md, conductor/product-guidelines.md, conductor/tech-stack.md, conductor/workflow.md, conductor/tracks.md, conductor/code_styleguides/

I will ask about: Project name, description, problem statement, target users, voice and tone, design principles, primary languages, frontend/backend frameworks, database, infrastructure, TDD strictness, commit strategy, code review policy, and style guide preferences.

best_practices: Complete all Q&A sections in one session for consistent documentation
Review generated files and customize templates as needed
Use the --resume flag if setup is interrupted mid-process
anti_patterns: Skipping sections without understanding their impact on generated docs
Providing inaccurate tech stack information leading to mismatched style guides
Not reviewing generated documentation for accuracy

faq.question: What is Conductor?
faq.answer: Conductor is a project management framework that uses documentation tracks to organize work. This skill helps set up the initial documentation structure.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my source code?
faq.answer: No. This skill only creates documentation files in the conductor/ directory. It does not modify any existing source code.

faq.question: Can I resume setup if interrupted?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --resume flag or the resume option to continue from where you left off. The skill tracks progress in conductor/setup_state.json.

faq.question: What files does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates conductor/index.md, product.md, product-guidelines.md, tech-stack.md, workflow.md, tracks.md, and code style guides.

faq.question: How does it detect my tech stack?
faq.answer: For existing projects, it scans for package.json, requirements.txt, go.mod, or Cargo.toml to infer languages and frameworks.

faq.question: Can I customize the generated documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill generates template files that you can edit afterward to match your specific project needs.
